title: Practice Rooms
openingLine: Hi, I was at MEA last weekend and I saw your practice room booths. I'd love to add some at my school — can someone come out and tell me whether they'd even fit and what we'd need to do?
customerContext: You're excited about what you saw at the conference. You haven't talked to your principal or district yet — you're in the 'gathering information' phase. You don't have a budget but you do have a vision: you've been begging for individual practice space for years and you want to make a strong case.

hiddenInfo.reveal: No budget allocated. I'm gathering info to build a case to the principal.
Realistically next school year, maybe the year after, depending on funding.
Principal first, then the district facilities team, then probably the school board for anything large.
I have some open floor space in the band room that could fit 2-3 booths. Or we could carve up an unused practice area down the hall.
No one. This is a freestanding equipment purchase, not a renovation.
I have 140 kids in band and they need way more individual practice access than we currently have.
I haven't thought about Studio — only saw the booths at the show.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Requested layout from facilities or offered field measurement
Researched purchase history before the visit
Asked about needs, budget, and timeline up front
Asked if they're working with a consultant or architect

Solution Framing.behavior: Discussed National Contracts vs. competitive bidding
Discussed Wenger Advantage Guarantee for SoundLok
Offered help if the project is over budget

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Probed for Studio option as an alternative or complement
Probed for adjacent storage or rehearsal space needs

BANT.behavior: Identified all decision makers (principal, district, school board)
